title: Multiple Conditions (AND/OR)
description: Combine multiple rules to narrow down searches.

slides.title: Layering the Filters 🥞
slides.content:
# Too many options

What if you want to find users who live in 'Canada' AND are over 18 years old? One condition isn't enough!

We can combine requirements using the logical operators **`AND`** and **`OR`**.

slides.title: The AND Operator 🤝
slides.content:
## Strict Rules

**`AND`** means that **both** conditions must be true for the row to be selected.

`SELECT * FROM users WHERE country = 'Canada' AND age > 18;`

If you are from Canada but only 16 years old... you get rejected! If you are 30 but from the USA... you get rejected!

quizData.question: If I write `WHERE color = 'Red' AND type = 'SUV'`, which car will be selected?
quizData.explanation: Because AND is used, BOTH conditions must be perfectly satisfied for the vehicle to be included.

slides.title: The OR Operator 👐
slides.content:
## Flexible Rules

**`OR`** means that **only one** of the conditions needs to be true!

`SELECT * FROM users WHERE country = 'Canada' OR country = 'USA';`

If you live in Canada? Welcome! If you live in the USA? Welcome!

quizData.question: If I write `WHERE category = 'Pizza' OR category = 'Burger'`, what gets selected?

options.text: Only items that are somehow both a Pizza and a Burger

quizData.explanation: OR is inclusive. If a row satisfies the first condition, or the second, it is allowed through the filter.

slides.title: Using Parentheses ( ) 🛡️
slides.content:
## Controlling Logic

When combining `AND` and `OR`, you must use parentheses `()` to tell the database which rule comes first (just like in Math). 

`WHERE (country = 'USA' OR country = 'UK') AND age >= 21;`

This says: Firstly, you must be from the USA or UK. Secondly, no matter which one of those you are from, you MUST be 21+.
